value.found_quote: Administrators can prevent this mode by setting `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode` to `"disable"` in [managed settings](#managed-settings)... To prevent `bypassPermissions` or `auto` mode from being used, set `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode` or `permissions.disableAutoMode` to `"disable"` in any [settings file](/en/settings#settings-files). These are most useful in [managed settings](#managed-settings) where they cannot be overridden.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes "managed settings" as a mechanism by which administrators can distribute and lock down permission policies enterprise-wide. It confirms that administrators can disable bypassPermissions mode and auto mode via `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode` and `permissions.disableAutoMode` settings, and that these settings in managed settings "cannot be overridden." The page also notes that permission settings "can be checked into version control and distributed to all developers in your organization." This substantiates all key aspects of the claim: enterprise-wide distribution, locking down policies, disabling bypass and auto modes, and restricting which permission modes are available.
